title: Chain of Custody Flow
description: Understand (Bloom L2) the chain of custody by stepping through each evidence transfer from crime scene to courtroom and explaining the documentation required at every handoff.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Chain of Custody
Evidence Handling
Forensic Admissibility
bloomLevel: Understand (L2) — explain the chain of custody and the documentation required at each transfer.
chapter_title: Crime Scene Investigation and Evidence Collection

educational.topic: Chain of Custody and Evidence Documentation
learningObjectives: Explain the six stages an evidence item passes through from collection to courtroom presentation.
Identify the documentation required at each transfer: item ID, date, location, condition notes, and signature.
Reason about why a single broken link can make evidence inadmissible in court.
educational.prerequisites: Basic idea of a crime scene and physical evidence; concept of a courtroom trial

pedagogical.interactionPattern: Step-through workflow: Next/Prev navigation across six custody stations with an always-visible detail panel and a Break the Chain toggle
pedagogical.assessment: Students narrate the responsible party and required documentation at each step, then test the consequence of a broken link
pedagogical.modelType: State-machine workflow of sequential custody transfers
